---
title: J. Upload to BigPicture
---

Encrypt and submit your validated bundle to the NBIS **staging** inbox with
**`bp-submitter`**. This is the final step of the operator journey.

::: {.callout-note}
## This is a staging (test) upload

The A–J manual is a test submission, so this step uploads to BigPicture's
**staging** environment — the `s3cmd-inbox.conf` NBIS provides for the manual
points there. The signed
[Terms of Use form](https://bigpictureeu.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/wp1/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B5914340E-2976-4454-BD7A-1DE64825EDC5%7D&file=BigPicture%20ToU%20v2.6.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true)
*(internal)* is **not** needed for a staging test — you only sign and send it to
[submit@bigpicture.eu](mailto:submit@bigpicture.eu) before a **production**
submission.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Precondition: NBIS receiver credentials

NBIS provides two files before you can upload. Because the A–J manual is a
**staging** test, place both in your staging credentials directory
(`PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_CREDS_DIR_STAGING`, default `certs/staging`) — a subfolder
of the `certs/` directory you set up in [F. TLS + credentials](tls-credentials.qmd).
For a real **production** submission the equivalent files go in
`PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_CREDS_DIR_PRODUCTION` (default `certs/production`) instead, so
the two instances never share credentials:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `s3cmd-inbox.conf` | S3 connection configuration for the NBIS inbox |
| `crypt4gh_bp_key.pub` | NBIS receiver public key for encryption |

Contact NBIS if you do not have these files. Do **not** commit them to version
control — they are already covered by the `.gitignore`.
:::

## 1. Dry run

Run a dry run first. It validates your credentials, bundle path, and encryption
readiness without transferring any data.

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows
In **Git Bash**, run:
```bash
make upload-dry-run DATASET=<project_dir>
```
## macOS
```bash
make upload-dry-run DATASET=<project_dir>
```
## Linux
```bash
make upload-dry-run DATASET=<project_dir>
```
:::

Replace `<project_dir>` with the project directory name — it is the value after
`Project:` in the `make export-check` output from [H. Run a submission](run-submission.qmd),
for example `DATASET_test-submission.10494c10-0d6b-4103-89e0-4909ad6e23af`.

`make upload-dry-run` defaults to **staging**. To dry-run against production
credentials instead, add `TARGET=production`.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

A passing dry run starts with:

```
Dry-run upload of '<project_dir>' to staging (zero bytes) ...
```

and exits 0 with no `ERROR` lines. Fix any reported errors before uploading.
:::

## 2. Upload

When the dry run passes, run the real upload. `make upload` **requires** a
`TARGET` — it never guesses which instance to push to. The A–J manual is a
staging test, so use `TARGET=staging`:

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows
In **Git Bash**, run:
```bash
make upload DATASET=<project_dir> TARGET=staging
```
## macOS
```bash
make upload DATASET=<project_dir> TARGET=staging
```
## Linux
```bash
make upload DATASET=<project_dir> TARGET=staging
```
:::

For a real submission, use `TARGET=production` instead — it reads the separate
`PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_CREDS_DIR_PRODUCTION` credentials:

```bash
make upload DATASET=<project_dir> TARGET=production
```

::: {.callout-warning}
## Use `--threads 1` (the default)

NBIS has explicitly asked submitters not to use parallel upload threads.
`make upload` enforces `--threads 1` by default.

If NBIS has explicitly told you that more threads are allowed for your site,
you can override:

```bash
make upload DATASET=<project_dir> TARGET=staging THREADS=4
```
:::

## Notify NBIS
Once your submission is completed, notify NBIS by [sending an email](mailto:{{< var email.helpdesk >}}). The subject of the email should be in the format of: `submission - DATASET_{IDENTIFIER}`, for example `submission - DATASET_test-submission.10494c10-0d6b-4103-89e0-4909ad6e23af`.

The email should contain:

- Name of the uploader
- Name of the dataset

## Resumable uploads

`bp-submitter` tracks progress in a local SQLite database at
`${PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_STATE}/upload.db` (default:
`$PIPELINE_ROOT/submitter-state/upload.db`). If an upload is interrupted,
re-run the same `make upload DATASET=<project_dir> TARGET=<staging|production>`
command — it resumes from where it left off.

To start over (for example, after replacing a corrected bundle), delete
`upload.db` from your `PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_STATE` directory before re-running.

## If something goes wrong

- **`TARGET is required: ...`** — `make upload` will not run without a target.
  Re-run with `TARGET=staging` (the manual) or `TARGET=production`.
- **`Invalid TARGET '<x>': expected 'staging' or 'production'`** — the `TARGET`
  value is mistyped. Use exactly `staging` or `production`.
- **`Missing creds file: .../s3cmd-inbox.conf`** or **`Missing creds file:
  .../crypt4gh_bp_key.pub`** — one of the NBIS-provided files is absent from the
  credentials directory for the `TARGET` you chose (`certs/staging` for
  `TARGET=staging`, `certs/production` for `TARGET=production`). Place the missing
  file in that directory and retry.
- **`DATASET not set`** — re-run with `DATASET=<project_dir>`.
- **`Dataset dir not found`** — the project directory name does not match what
  is under `PIPELINE_OUTBOX_DIR`. Confirm the name from `make export-check`
  output (step H).
- **Connection or authentication errors** — your `s3cmd-inbox.conf` may be
  expired or misconfigured. Contact NBIS for an updated file.
- **Upload stalls and does not resume** — delete `upload.db` from
  `PIPELINE_SUBMITTER_STATE` and retry from the beginning.
